content: Vaccines help your body prepare to fight certain diseases without you having to get sick first. They do this by introducing a harmless piece or a weakened form of a germ into your body. Your immune system then recognizes this as a threat and learns how to attack it. This “training” means if you encounter the real disease later, your immune system can respond much faster and stronger. Without this preparation, your body might take longer to react, allowing the disease to cause more harm. Vaccines are tested thoroughly to ensure they are safe and effective before being approved for use. While protection can sometimes decrease over time, booster shots can help maintain your immunity. In short, vaccines train your immune system like a practice drill, so it’s ready to defend you quickly and effectively.
